programme.title: Mad Men
programme.sub-title: Shut the Door. Have a Seat
programme.desc: With another sale on the horizon, Sterling, Cooper, and Draper convince Lane Pryce to fire them and then join them as they start up a new advertising firm; at home, Betty follows through with her plans to divorce Don.

programme.title: Mad Men
programme.desc: Roger attempts to find a girlfriend for Don; Don's secretive demeanor results in an unfavorable interview by a reporter.

programme.title: Mad Men
programme.sub-title: Christmas Comes but Once a Year
programme.desc: The firm reluctantly throws a Christmas party for its most important client; Don prepares for his first Christmas away from his children and makes an indiscretion.

programme.title: Mad Men
programme.desc: Joan is trying to start a family with Greg, but her work schedule and his impending army commitments make things difficult; Don takes a trip to California to see Anna and meets her niece, who delivers some unsettling news.

programme.title: Lucky Hank
programme.desc: Hank rants against Railton College, calling it "Mediocrity's Capitol," and the administration is pressured to fire him; Hank and Lily contemplate a future outside of Railton.

programme.title: Lucky Hank
programme.desc: When George Saunders visits Railton College, the professors scramble to impress the celebrated writer, while Hank is reminded of his own failed writing career; Hank and Lily anticipate an announcement from their daughter Julie.

programme.title: Lucky Hank
programme.desc: Hank spirals when a storage pod full of his father's belongings is dropped in his front yard; the professors spiral when rumors of budget cuts threaten their tenured jobs.

programme.title: Lucky Hank
programme.desc: Amid firing rumors, the professors look to the union to protect their jobs, while Hank tries a different approach; Lily interviews for a job at a prestigious New York prep school.

programme.title: Lucky Hank
programme.desc: Hank and Lily host the annual English department dinner; Lily has a decision to make; Hank's past involving his troubled relationship with his father catches up to him.

programme.title: Lucky Hank
programme.desc: Hank and Tony travel to an academic conference, where their differing philosophies on work and life cause friction; Lily mediates trouble in Julie and Russell's marriage.

programme.title: Lucky Hank
programme.desc: Hank contends with the return of his father to Railton; Lily hunts for apartments in New York; Julie looks to Hank for help in Lily's absence.

programme.title: Lucky Hank
programme.desc: Hank is faced with an impossible decision at work, as his department's jobs are on the line; Lily settles into a new normal in New York.

programme.title: The Audacity
programme.desc: Duncan, almost tech titan, must prove he and his company are F-able; JoAnne provides therapy with benefits; Orson lands from Baltimore; the party for Dr. Green ends in a cat burglary.

programme.title: Scrublands
programme.desc: Disenchanted investigative journalist Martin Scarsden is told by his boss to write a follow-up piece a year after a mass shooting of five parishioners by a charismatic young priest in the small rural town of Riversend.

programme.title: Scrublands
programme.desc: Martin Scarsden decides to stay on in Riversend to dig deeper into the mystery of Byron Swift, a well-liked priest who suddenly murdered five of his parishioners.

programme.title: Scrublands
programme.desc: At teenager Allen's wake, Martin learns that the boy was not as innocent as first portrayed, with Robbie telling him he was persuaded by Byron to drop drug charges against him.

programme.title: Scrublands
programme.desc: Martin believes he has found the link between the shooting victims and asks for Robbie's help in accessing the case files; a further clue implicates one of the victims.

programme.title: The Numbers Station
programme.desc: Emerson, a disgraced CIA agent, guards Katherine, a code operator, in a top secret CIA installation in Britain. When heavily armed assailants lay siege to the compound, Emerson and Katherine have to stop the deadly plot before it's too late.

programme.title: Once Upon a Time in Venice
programme.desc: To get his dog back, private eye Steve Ford forges an unlikely alliance with Spyder, the leader of a local gang. With help from his best friend, Steve pulls out the big guns to retrieve Spyder's missing cash and cocaine and save his beloved pooch.

programme.title: Mad Men
programme.desc: Pete gets some bad news and then some good news, which affects Peggy more than she can say; Allison can't keep her feelings under wraps.

programme.title: Mad Men
programme.desc: Don and Pete try to land a new Japanese client, but Roger's racist attitude may hand the lucrative account to a rival; Don has to read deeply and think quickly to salvage things.

programme.title: Mad Men
programme.desc: Don and the agency are up for an award for their Glo Coat ad; Roger reflects on how he first met Don.

programme.title: Mad Men
programme.desc: An impending deadline leaves the firm in disarray; Don makes Peggy stay late to work on a Samsonite ad, missing a birthday dinner with her boyfriend, Mark.

programme.title: Mad Men
programme.desc: Don attempts to regain control over his life through physical changes and journal writing; Don's persistence with Faye results in an impromptu dinner date.

programme.title: Mad Men
programme.desc: Peggy is forced to face some unpleasant facts about a client's discriminatory business practices; Don and Faye's burgeoning relationship is tested when Sally runs away from home and turns up at the office.
